conjecture_title: Question on extremal mutually annihilating pairs (Theorem 2.4)
paper_title: Combinatorics in the exterior algebra and the Bollobás Two Families Theorem
summary: No follow-up paper characterizing all extremal mutually annihilating pairs U ⊆ ∧^r V, W ⊆ ∧^s V with dim U · dim W = C(n-1,r-1)·C(n-1,s-1) was found in the literature. The source paper was published in the Journal of the London Mathematical Society in 2021 (from the 2019 preprint). A related 2024 paper by Bulavka (arXiv:2406.17857) answers a Hilton-Milner-type extension question posed by Scott and Wilmer, but it concerns self-annihilating subspaces and an additive bound, not the multiplicative extremal characterization of Theorem 2.4.
search_queries: mutually annihilating subspaces exterior algebra extremal characterization Scott Wilmer
arXiv 1907.06019 Bollobás exterior algebra mutually annihilating pairs
exterior algebra "mutually annihilating" extremal characterization "wedge product" subspaces 2021 2022 2023 2024
notes: The conjecture is from 2020 (6 years old), so medium rather than high confidence. The only post-2020 paper found that addresses Scott-Wilmer questions in this setting is arXiv:2406.17857 (Bulavka, Transactions of the London Mathematical Society, 2025), which answers the Hilton-Milner extension for self-annihilating subspaces — a distinct open question from the same paper. The specific extremal characterization for the product bound dim U · dim W = C(n-1,r-1)·C(n-1,s-1) remains unresolved in all indexed literature found.
